name: cabin-trapper
summary: A fur-trapper's one-room cabin — skinning work dominates the space, pelts hang everywhere, and the smell of tallow lingers.

primary_palette.note: work-first cabin — the pelt trade pays the rent
peak season — two racks in use, curing hides everywhere
winter-bound trapper, cooking and curing over the same fire
the trapper is a craftsman — making and repairing traps between runs

story_prompts: empty — trapper is three days out on the line, check the ledger for the route
recently abandoned after a bad encounter — a partial skin on the rack, tools dropped
occupied year-round by a taciturn woman who trades only in Spring
a pair of brothers share it in rotation — two bedrolls, two knives, one disagreement
trap-making workshop serving several neighboring lines — more workbench than home

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: low (0.2-0.3) — hearth-lit with one tallow lamp

anti_patterns: no refined furniture — no upholstered chairs, no painted cabinets
hides go on walls and racks, not the floor
avoid neat organization — trapping is a messy trade, let the scatter show it
